title: elaeocarpus obovatus subsp. obovatus
intro: Elaeocarpus obovatus subsp. obovatus, also known as the Blue Quandong, is a shrub or small tree species native to Australia, characterized by its small, blue or purple flowers and edible fruit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus obovatus subsp. obovatus include the potential treatment of various conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for colds and fever. Elaeocarpus obovatus subsp. obovatus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, tincture, and capsule, which can be made by infusing the leaves and fruit in hot water or using a solvent to extract the active compounds.
